title: 10 best herbal creams for asthma

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with asthma because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, Echinacea purpurea, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties help to relax constricted airway muscles, allowing for improved airflow and reduced wheezing. As a result, individuals who use herbal Kansas coneflower creams may experience relief from asthma symptoms, including coughing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier for asthmatic individuals to breathe.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of the cream help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Expectorant: The expectorant properties of the cream help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier for asthmatic individuals to cough up and expel excess mucus, reducing congestion and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and polyphenols, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for soothing respiratory issues like asthma.
Seeds: Containing a unique combination of fatty acids, antioxidants, and phytosterols, seeds of Kansas coneflower plant can help reduce inflammation and bronchial constriction associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots and dry them completely within 24 hours of collection.
Grind 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered echinacea with 100 grams of beeswax pellets, 50 grams of coconut oil, and 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 160°f over low heat while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted within 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, and whip with an immersion blender or whisk until smooth and creamy within 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur creams helps with asthma because they contain extracts from the butterbur plant, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to relax the airways. The plant's active compounds, such as petasin and isopetasin, have been shown to inhibit the production of leukotrienes, a type of chemical mediator that contributes to bronchial constriction in people with asthma. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the airway muscles, butterbur creams can help to alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PA's): Butterbur cream contains PA's, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relieve asthmatic symptoms by reducing inflammation in the airways and widening airway passages.

Isoalantolactone: This compound has been shown to inhibit the production of leukotrienes, powerful mediators of allergic responses that can exacerbate asthma symptoms, thereby providing relief from wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
Allantoin: Allantoin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce airway inflammation and congestion associated with asthma, making it easier for patients to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the butterbur plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the airways and relieving asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the butterbur plant contain compounds that help to relax bronchial muscles and open up airways, providing relief from asthma attacks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh petasites hybridus leaves and stems, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pests.
Dry the harvested petasites hybridus in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels below 10%.
Combine 100g of dried petasites hybridus with 300ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 40°c.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with asthma because they contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated airways. When inhaled, these mucilages help to reduce inflammation and excess mucus production, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, marshmallow creams can also help to loosen and expel stubborn phlegm from the lungs, further alleviating symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions. By providing a protective barrier in the airways, marshmallow creams can help to alleviate coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow roots contain a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with asthma.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow creams contain various anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in marshmallow creams help to thin and loosen mucus in the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots provide a soothing and protective barrier for irritated respiratory tissues, helping to ease breathing difficulties associated with asthma.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, the rhyzomes help to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Blend 100g of the cleaned marigold flowers with 500ml of distilled water in a blender.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the cooled marigold infusion and heat it to 80°c for 15 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and set before transferring it to sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with asthma because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, it helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms such as wheezing and coughing. Additionally, licorice creams have been shown to help relax the airway muscles, further opening up airways and allowing for more efficient airflow. Overall, licorice creams can provide relief from asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving lung function.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals, such as leukotrienes and prostaglandins, which contribute to bronchospasm and airway inflammation in asthma patients.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in licorice cream may help to stabilize mast cells, reducing the release of histamine and other allergy mediators that can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Phytosterols: Licorice creams contain phytosterols, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce airway inflammation and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing bronchial inflammation and relieving asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contains isoflavones, which have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 100ml of coconut oil and 50ml of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 60°c before adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil and 5ml of aloe vera gel.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours before use, allowing the cream to thicken and mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with asthma because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to soothe and calm the airways. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the herbal extracts present in Tasmanian blue gum creams have natural bronchodilatory effects, which can help relax and dilate the airways, making it simpler for asthmatic individuals to take deep breaths without experiencing wheezing or coughing.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's essential oil contains compounds like cineole, which helps reduce nasal congestion and inflammation associated with asthma, allowing for easier breathing.
Bronchodilatory effects: The eucalyptol present in the oil relaxes airway muscles, widening bronchi and improving airflow to the lungs, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.
Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms and reduce lung function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and constrict airways, making them effective in treating asthma symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contains flavonoids and terpenoids that help to relax airway muscles and improve breathing function, making it a useful component in asthma creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the ground eucalyptus powder with 100g of beeswax and 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before adding 20ml of distilled water and mixing well.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator for a minimum of 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and relax the airways. The herb's essential oils, particularly thymol, help reduce swelling and constrict blood vessels, making it easier for air to flow freely. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties combat infections that can exacerbate asthma symptoms. As a result, topical application of herbal thyme creams can provide quick relief from wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, offering a natural alternative to conventional treatments for asthma management.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms and reduce lung function.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and constriction of the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Bronchodilatory properties: Thyme's flavonoids, including luteolin and apigenin, can help relax airway muscles, widening airways and improving lung function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, thyme roots help to soothe and calm airways, reducing inflammation associated with asthma.
Seeds: Containing antioxidants and flavonoids, thyme seeds help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from well-drained soil rich in organic matter at dawn.
Dry the thyme leaves by spreading them on paper towels for 4-6 hours or using a food dehydrator at low heat.
Combine 50g of dried thyme leaves with 200ml of coconut oil and infuse overnight at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of sweet almond oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler or microwave for 30 seconds, stirring frequently, until fully incorporated and cooled to a spreadable consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with asthma because they offer a natural and effective way to ease respiratory issues. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion. This can help to relieve symptoms such as wheezing and coughing, while also opening up airways to improve breathing. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint may help to reduce inflammation in the lungs, further alleviating asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol's decongestant and expectorant properties help to thin mucus, making it easier to cough up and reduce bronchial constriction, thus alleviating asthma symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a common underlying issue in asthma patients.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of peppermint cream can help relax smooth muscle spasms in the airways, reducing bronchial constriction and alleviating symptoms like wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation associated with asthma.
Leaves: Contain menthol, a natural decongestant that helps to ease breathing and relieve congestion caused by asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market for further processing.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm dry place for at least two weeks ensuring complete drying.
Combine dried peppermint leaves and 20 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl for melting.
Add 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 5 milliliters of coconut oil to the melted mixture stirring well for uniform blending.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into sterilized containers and store at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with asthma because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the airways, reducing swelling and constriction. The active ingredient, gingerol, has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that trigger bronchospasm. Additionally, herbal extracts in these creams have antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, further alleviating symptoms of asthma. As a result, regular application of herbal ginger creams can provide relief from wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of asthma.
Expectorant properties: Ginger's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal ginger creams' antioxidant activity helps protect the airways from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing compounds like gingerols and shogaols, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds, roots of the ginger plant possess anti-asthmatic properties that can soothe respiratory tract inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root and peel it to obtain a sufficient amount of zingiber officinale.
Dry the peeled ginger root in a warm place for at least 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried ginger root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Mix the ground ginger powder with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler setup on low heat for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to an airtight container for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with asthma because they contain the natural compound parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways. This can help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath associated with asthma attacks. Additionally, feverfew's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue can help improve lung function and make breathing easier. By reducing inflammation and improving lung function, feverfew creams can provide relief from asthma symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew contains parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which can help relieve asthma symptoms by reducing airway constriction and inflammation.

Feverfew flavonoids: The flavonoids present in feverfew cream have antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which is a contributing factor to asthma development and exacerbation.
Terpenes: Feverfew contains terpenes, such as camphor and borneol, which can help relax bronchial muscles and reduce spasms, thus alleviating symptoms of asthma like wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the lungs and airways associated with asthma.
Leaves: Containing parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that has bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing for asthma patients.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 20 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground tanacetum parthenium with 100 milliliters of jojoba oil in a small saucepan and heat it over low heat for 15 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for an additional 30 minutes to allow the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, squeezing out as much oil as possible from the herb residue.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before whipping it with an electric mixer until it reaches a creamy consistency suitable for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with asthma because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the airways and alleviate congestion. The cream's moisturizing properties also soothe dry, irritated skin, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, aloe vera's natural antihistamine effects can help to calm allergic reactions that can exacerbate asthma symptoms. By reducing inflammation and soothing the airways, herbal aloe creams provide natural relief from asthma attacks and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and isoaloin, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing.

Mucilages and gelling agents: Aloe vera gel contains mucilages and gelling agents that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by asthma triggers like pollution or allergens.

Antioxidants and immunomodulators: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants like vitamins C and E, as well as immunomodulatory compounds like acemannan, which help reduce oxidative stress and modulate the immune response to prevent excessive inflammation and airway constriction in asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant are rich in vitamins and minerals that help soothe and calm respiratory tract inflammation, making them ideal for treating asthma symptoms.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, providing relief from asthma attacks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves weighing at least 100 grams containing gel content of 90%.
Purify the harvested aloe vera gel using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove impurities and excess water.
Blend one tablespoon of dried ginger powder with two tablespoons of aloe vera gel in a small bowl for three minutes until well-combined.
Add five drops of eucalyptus essential oil and stir the mixture clockwise for four minutes allowing oils to meld together.
Transfer the cream to an airtight glass jar and store it at room temperature for 24 hours before using within seven days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with asthma because they contain natural extracts that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways. The creams also contain mucolytic agents that break down mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion. Additionally, the antioxidants present in maidenhair tree creams help to protect the lungs from further damage caused by free radicals. By addressing these underlying issues, maidenhair tree creams can provide relief for asthma sufferers and improve their overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which may alleviate symptoms of asthma.
Bronchodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and increase bronchial diameter, which may help improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
Inflammation reduction: Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, a common characteristic of asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe bronchial tubes and reduce inflammation associated with asthma.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients, contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from trusted sources ensuring potency and quality.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 100g of sweet almond oil with 50g of beeswax in a heat-proof glass bowl and melt over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 20g of ground ginkgo biloba to the melted mixture and stir until well combined allowing the ingredients to infuse for 30 minutes.
Pour the infused mixture into sterilized jars and let cool completely before use or storing in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with asthma because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The mucilage in slippery elm forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by asthma. This natural barrier also helps to loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce airway constriction, allowing for smoother airflow and improved lung function.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Slippery elm cream contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and coats the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by asthma.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal slippery elm cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in slippery elm cream help protect the lungs from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms and lung damage over time.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated airways, making them an ideal component for relieving asthma symptoms.
Barks: The barks of slippery elm contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, easing breathing difficulties associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and flowers in early morning for maximum potency then rinse thoroughly.
Combine 20g of dried ulmus rubra bark and 10g of dried echinacea angustifolia root in a heatproof glass bowl.
Add 200ml of cold-pressed olive oil to the mixture then place in a double boiler for 6 hours.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding solids and allowing it cool to room temperature.
Mix the cooled infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil whipping until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with asthma because they contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways. The anti-inflammatory properties of frankincense cream can help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and coughing, making it easier for individuals with asthma to breathe. Additionally, frankincense has natural antispasmodic properties that can help relax bronchial muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. By applying frankincense cream topically, individuals with asthma may experience improved lung function and reduced reliance on medication.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in frankincense help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Expectorant: Frankincense's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Frankincense roots are rich in boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties that help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the frankincense tree contains olivetolic acid, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest boswellia serrata gum resin from mature trees and dry it thoroughly in the shade for seven days.
Combine one tablespoon of dried boswellia serrata gum resin with two tablespoons of coconut oil and heat them gently over low flame for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove any impurities or residue from the oil.

Mix one teaspoon of beeswax pellets with three drops of lavender essential oil in a double boiler until melted and well combined, then gradually add the boswellia-coconut oil mixture while stirring constantly for 10 minutes.
Pour the blended mixture into clean glass jars, let it cool and solidify at room temperature for two hours before applying topically as needed for asthma relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with asthma because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root cause of asthma symptoms. Curcumin reduces inflammation in the airways, alleviating wheezing and shortness of breath, while also suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals that exacerbate asthma attacks. Additionally, turmeric's natural antioxidants help to stabilize lung tissue and reduce oxidative stress, promoting overall respiratory health and providing long-term relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A powerful anti-inflammatory compound found in turmeric, which helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, a common characteristic of asthma.
Cineole: A natural essential oil present in turmeric, which has been shown to relax bronchial muscles and improve lung function, making it easier to breathe.

Alpha-hydroxy fatty acids: Fatty acids found in turmeric that have been linked to anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lungs from damage caused by oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, making it effective in alleviating asthma symptoms.
Roots: The roots contain essential oils and active compounds that help soothe respiratory issues and reduce inflammation in the airways, providing relief from asthma attacks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots measuring 50 grams for the extract.
Grind the turmeric roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milliliters of coconut oil with 10 grams of turmeric powder in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes under low stirring conditions.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and pour into sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng creams helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways. The ginsenosides present in ginseng have been shown to relax bronchial muscles, making it easier for air to pass through and reducing symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath. Additionally, ginseng creams may also help to improve lung function and increase oxygenation, leading to improved overall respiratory health and reduced asthma severity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Bronchodilatory effects: Ginseng extracts have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and increase bronchial diameter, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginseng creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a hallmark of asthma and can lead to symptoms such as wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginseng plant are used to make medicinal creams for asthma because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help relieve respiratory congestion.
Barks: The barks of the ginseng plant are used to make medicinal creams for asthma because they contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried panax ginseng roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground panax ginseng powder with coconut oil in a 1:3 ratio by weight, adding vitamin e oil as an antioxidant at 5% concentration.
Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler until it reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it with a hand mixer or whisk until it becomes light and fluffy for 15 minutes.
Fill clean glass jars with the cooled herbal cream, labeling each jar with the date of preparation and storage time, which is up to six months in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with asthma because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to soothe and relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction. The creams also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, which can alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and coughing. Additionally, elder creams have been shown to increase the production of surfactant, a natural lubricant that helps to reduce bronchial irritation and promote healthy breathing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like anthocyanins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.

Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate asthma symptoms by damaging lung tissue and reducing the body's natural defense mechanisms.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in elder cream act as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve respiratory inflammation associated with asthma.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing asthmatic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers in the morning for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or sun drying method for at least 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried sambucus nigra flowers with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids and preserving the infused oil for use as a cream base.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with asthma because they contain compounds that have a bronchodilatory effect, which means they help relax the airways and improve lung function. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, wild marjoram has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, further alleviating symptoms of asthma.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Wild marjoram cream helps relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Expectorant property: The cream's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with asthma.
Anti-inflammatory property: Wild marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to chronic asthmatic conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild marjoram plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of asthma.
Buds: The buds of the wild marjoram plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, helping to alleviate wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves and dry them properly in the shade for several hours or overnight.
Grind dried origanum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Mix 2 tablespoons of ground origanum vulgare with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a consistent texture.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool slightly before pouring it into sterilized jars and allowing it to solidify completely at room temperature within 30-45 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm airway inflammation. The cream's active compounds, such as gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), help to reduce bronchial constriction and improve lung function. Additionally, borage creams' antispasmodic effects help to relax airway muscles, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing symptoms. As a result, borage creams provide natural relief from asthma symptoms, offering an alternative or complementary approach to traditional medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Borage cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), can help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a major contributor to asthma symptoms.

Bronchodilatory properties: The herb's constituents, including borage oil and its derivatives, have been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe by opening up airway passages.

Antioxidant activity: Borage cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is important in managing chronic lung diseases like asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated airways, making it an effective ingredient in asthma relief creams.
Flowers: Abundant in flavonoids and saponins, these compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce bronchial constriction and ease breathing difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers for maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the plant material by air-drying or using a food dehydrator at 60°f for 24 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried borago officinalis with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until it reaches a temperature of 160°f and allows for infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and store it at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways. The cream's active compounds, such as camphor and eucalyptus oil, work to ease congestion and open up air passages, making it easier for patients to breathe. Additionally, sage's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscles in the lungs and airways, further alleviating symptoms of asthma. By using herbal sage creams regularly, asthmatics may experience fewer attacks and improved lung function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal sage creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of asthma.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms and damage lung tissue over time.

Bronchodilatory effects: The volatile oils present in sage cream, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to dilate airway passages and improve lung function, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to create creams that soothe bronchial inflammation and reduce symptoms of asthma.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of sesquiterpenes, rhyzomes help to relax airway muscles and open up constricted airways, alleviating asthma attacks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves for subsequent infusion.
Combine 30g of the infused leaves with 100ml of carrier oil and let it macerate for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids immediately.
Mix 10ml of the extracted herbal oil with 90ml of beeswax and heat to 65°c for melting.
Pour the cooled herbal salve into sterilized containers and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm airways. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, lavender's calming effects on the mind and body can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of asthma attacks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: English lavender creams contain linalool, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.

Calming and sedative effects: Lavender's calming properties can help to relax the bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can exacerbate asthma symptoms, allowing for easier breathing.

Antispasmodic effects: The antispasmodic compounds present in English lavender creams can help to relieve bronchial spasms, which are a common symptom of asthma attacks, by relaxing the smooth muscle tissue in the airways.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are valued for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing respiratory issues associated with asthma.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain flavonoids and volatile oils that help reduce inflammation and spasms in the airways, providing relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried lavender flowers lavandula angustifolia for its calming properties.
Combine the dried lavender with 1 cup of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm water bath at 100°f to 110°f for 4 hours.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean container and discard solids.
Mix the resulting lavender-infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for asthma are topical preparations made from natural herbs, plants, and botanicals that provide relief from asthma symptoms. These creams work by reducing inflammation, opening airways, and soothing bronchial spasms, thereby alleviating wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Examples of herbal creams that help with asthma include those containing eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme oils, which can be applied directly to the chest or back. By using these creams, people with asthma experience improved lung function, reduced medication usage, and enhanced quality of life, allowing them to live more freely without constant worry about their breathing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat asthma with herbal creams is to use topical preparations containing natural anti-inflammatory compounds like marshmallow root, licorice extract, and butcher's broom. These herbs help reduce airway inflammation and constriction, providing quick relief from asthma symptoms. Regular application of these creams can also improve lung function and reduce reliance on pharmaceutical medications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to asthma that are treated with herbal creams are chronic bronchitis, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and respiratory allergies. These conditions often exhibit symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, which can be alleviated by topical application of herbal creams containing ingredients like eucalyptus, peppermint, and chamomile.
